commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Needs human maintainer review of the package contract, final-head proof, and ordering with the overlapping gateway shrinkwrap PR; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security regression was found; the new npm/ClawHub artifact paths are fixed-hash package inputs and runtime activation still avoids package-manager installs. (sha=354bfc7648446a75d1aa132322ddb541c4e3be08)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds shrinkwrap and ClawHub npm-pack materialization for generated OpenClaw runtime plugin locks, regenerates supported plugin outputs, hardens validation scripts, and rewrites runtime-plugin docs.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The relevant evidence is generated lock output plus real Nix build/check proof, not a failing current-main reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs human maintainer review of the package contract, final-head proof, and ordering with the overlapping gateway shrinkwrap PR; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: feat: materialize shrinkwrapped runtime plugins
target.body:
# Human written summary:
The intent of this change is, as written by a human:
> all users want is to be able to use nix-openclaw with plugins the same way that they do with upstream openclaw, but declaratively. its that simple. we want to figure out how to implement that here in simplest possible way but no simpler; with a minimum of retarded shit and hacks

_The rest of this PR was written by GPT-5 Codex-unknown, running in the codex harness. Full environment + prompt history appear at the end._

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

# Maintainer Decisions
- Users still configure OpenClaw catalog runtime plugins with one shape: `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins = [ "id" ];`.
- nix-openclaw does not expose `npm:`, `clawhub:`, git, local-path, archive, or marketplace install strings as `runtimePlugins` inputs.
- Upstream install surfaces are resolved at lock-update time into immutable package roots, then built from Nix store artifacts.
- A catalog artifact is packageable when it has no runtime npm dependencies, bundles the declared runtime dependency roots in `node_modules`, or ships an `npm-shrinkwrap.json` that `fetchNpmDeps` can replay offline with a generated `npmDepsHash`.
- ClawHub is treated as a resolver, not a separate user-facing plugin class. If ClawHub resolves to a fixed npm-pack tarball with hash/integrity, it goes through the same runtime plugin builder.
- Home Manager activation and OpenClaw runtime still do not call npm, ClawHub, or `openclaw plugins install`.

# Changes
- Rewrites the README runtime-plugin section around concrete examples: bundled-in-gateway, npm with bundled deps, npm with no deps, npm with shrinkwrap, ClawHub, Codex/ACPX, currently unpackageable catalog plugins, and arbitrary third-party sources.
- Updates maintainer plugin docs and RFC 2 with the same upstream-command-to-Nix-config mapping.
- Hardens bundled dependency admission: bundled mode now requires every declared direct runtime dependency root to exist in bundled `node_modules`, instead of treating any bundled package root as enough.
- Keeps shrinkwrap mode as the materialized dependency path and keeps optional dependency handling platform-aware at install validation time.
- Aligns generator/install package-root collection around `lstatSync` so symlinked `node_modules` entries are not accidentally treated differently.
- Adds retry handling around npm/ClawHub JSON fetches after the updater hit a transient `EPIPE` during local `--check`.
- Regenerates the OpenClaw v2026.6.1 runtime plugin report: 34 supported rows, 4 skipped rows.

# User-Facing Support Contract
In this generated lock, users can select every emitted supported catalog id with the same API. The notable examples are:

- `brave`: npm catalog artifact with no runtime npm dependencies.
- `slack` and `discord`: npm catalog artifacts with bundled `node_modules`.
- `memory-lancedb`, `codex`, `acpx`, `copilot`, `tlon`: npm catalog artifacts with shrinkwrap materialization.
- `whatsapp` and `matrix`: ClawHub catalog artifacts that resolve to fixed npm-pack tarballs, then build through the shrinkwrap path.

The current real packageability gaps are Weixin, Yuanbao, and WeCom. Their published tarballs declare runtime dependencies but contain neither `npm-shrinkwrap.json` nor bundled `node_modules`, so upstream needs to publish shrinkwrap or bundled runtime dependencies before nix-openclaw can emit those ids. PixVerse is already emitted once; the remaining PixVerse skipped row is only a duplicate catalog row.

# Tests
- `node --check nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs` passed.
- `node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-install.mjs` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `./nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs --check` passed: 34 supported, 4 skipped.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-plugin-locks .#checks.aarch64-darwin.config-validity` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-brave` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-slack` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-memory-lancedb` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-whatsapp` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-acpx` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-codex` passed.
- `nix flake show --accept-flake-config` passed.
- `scripts/check-flake-lock-owners.sh` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci` passed before the final README wording-only fix.

# Local Review
- Docs subagent found one P3 gap: the README did not explicitly name Slack/Discord as the bundled `node_modules` example. Fixed by renaming that section and adding the bundled-deps sentence.
- Code/packageability subagent found no concrete issue in the final diff.
- Main-agent ClawSweeper-style pass fixed PR-context leakage in README wording and tightened stale generated-report wording.

# Risks
- The lock updater is heavier for maintainers because it resolves npm/ClawHub artifacts, computes npm dependency hashes, and probes materialization. User activation does not get this cost.
- This PR expands the supported runtime plugin package surface. That is intentional, but maintainers should review it as a package contract change.
- PR #100 overlaps at the gateway shrinkwrap layer. This PR keeps runtime plugin materialization separate; merge order still needs final-head CI/Garnix proof.

# Prompt History

## Environment
Harness: codex
Model: GPT-5 Codex
Thinking level: max
Terminal: zsh on macOS
System: macOS 26.5

## Prompts
| ISO-8601 | Prompt |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-06-05T16:20:00+02:00 | `Goal: design the next architecture step for nix-openclaw support of OpenClaw's upstream runtime npm/ClawHub plugin model. Do not patch repos yet. Do not create worktrees.` |
| 2026-06-05T16:40:00+02:00 | `all users want is to be able to use nix-openclaw with plugins the same way that they do with upstream openclaw, but declaratively. its that simple. we want to figure out how to implement that here in simplest possible way but no simpler; with a minimum of retarded shit and hacks` |
| 2026-06-05T16:55:00+02:00 | `what about clawhub does that shrinkwrap? the poitn isn't security the point is pacakging. if they have shrinkwwrapping then we have immutable installs OOTB and its easy, if not, then fuck off. right? go back to overall goal for the session: how do we make this work for our original north star of users being able to install plugins same way as in upstream?` |
| 2026-06-05T17:05:00+02:00 | `sure thats fine. okay then i think we have everyhting, simple as possible user config, as few knobs as possible, we support everything that has shrinkwrapped, we have a config, etc, this should be clear?` |
| 2026-06-05T17:05:30+02:00 | `lets create a worktree for implementing this and PR it. lets use [$session-goal-writer](/nix/store/hcks99ivrjhg2pg09lc97cqhd2ixh1w1-source/skills/session-goal-writer/SKILL.md) , success should be: written RFC that passes adversarial review (step one)) written execplan that passes adversarial review (step two) and written implementation and PR with green CI that also passes clawsweeper rubrics (locally [$clawsweeper-preflight](/Users/josh/.codex/skills/clawsweeper-preflight/SKILL.md) ) and remotely. is that enough info to start implementation while i go cook dinner?` |
| 2026-06-05T20:37:00+02:00 | `the docs as they stand in your PR are either wrong or misleading. they say we don't support a bunch of plugins when the whole point of this change was that we DO? any shrink wrapped plugin will work? so this is really not good enough. the whole point is that we can install all plugins (that are shrink wrapped) and tell end users that we have basically the same support contract as upstream. so either the docs are wrong or the feature isn't implemented as we discussed? i also wanna see a full adversarial review against all our criteria in session goal to ensure docs are fully coherent and up to date` |
| 2026-06-05T20:45:00+02:00 | `btw if some of the core plugins aren't packageable i wanna know why. do we have to shrinkwrap them? or must we create a PR to those plugins? (don't do it yet) also be aware of the other thread working on shrinkwrapping entire nix openclaw, there's some overlap there, i think you should merge first but let's see. also be aware that you might be able to learn things from those changes and vice versa so make sure to read the PR` |
| 2026-06-05T22:52:00+02:00 | `i added PR comments too. long story short: we need a full HUMAN FIRST DOCUMENTSTJON pass, there's too much AI slop that doesn't make sense for users and is poorly explained with too much commentary and not enough config examples for each use case also i still don't trust the code. i want it to be as simple as possible but no simpler. use session goal and try and do multiple passes of adversarial review to make docs more human and code more simple where possible. get subagents to check for you too` |
target.author: joshp123
